title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. caudatum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. caudatum, commonly known as the bracken fern, is a subspecies of the Pteridium aquilinum fern species, native to New Zealand, Australia, and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. caudatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically consumed in moderation due to potential toxicity and side effects associated with long-term consumption.
